title: Battleship Potemkin
summary: The Odessa Steps sequence compresses a massacre into a rhythm of short, colliding shots, the founding demonstration of montage as meaning.

title: The Passion of Joan of Arc
summary: Built almost entirely from close-ups of Renee Falconetti's face, trusting the smallest reaction to carry the entire trial.

title: Touch of Evil
summary: The opening tracking shot holds one unbroken take across a border crossing, building dread in real time with no cut to release it.

title: Breathless
summary: Godard cut within continuous shots rather than around them, turning the jump cut from an error into a restless, modern rhythm.

title: Double Indemnity
summary: A confession delivered in voiceover from the start frames the entire plot as a man narrating his own downfall.

title: Days of Heaven
summary: Shot almost entirely during the 'magic hour' after sunset, trading lighting control for a naturalism no studio lamp could match.

title: Intolerance
summary: Four separate historical stories are cross-cut together, accelerating toward a shared climax and establishing parallel editing at scale.

title: 2001: A Space Odyssey
summary: A thrown bone match-cuts directly into an orbiting spacecraft, collapsing millions of years of human tool-making into a single transition.

title: Festen
summary: Dogme #1, shot handheld on consumer video under the Dogme 95 vow of chastity, letting a family dinner's collapse feel unrehearsed and unsafe.

title: Jaws
summary: A malfunctioning shark prop forced the threat offscreen, so John Williams' theme and point-of-view shots did the work the creature could not.
